title: Boat Rental in Saronic Gulf: A 2026 Guide to Choosing Your Charter
html:
The Saronic Gulf, with its calm waters and proximity to Athens, is an ideal place to rent a boat. Departing from Alimos Marina, a half-day small motorboat costs from €180. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners to find the perfect vessel for your itinerary.

Where to set off in Saronic Gulf

The Saronic Gulf offers several well-served marinas and natural harbours. Alimos Marina, near Athens, is the largest and provides easy access to the islands. Flisvos Marina, a short drive from the city centre, offers luxury berths. Further south, the port of Poros island is a popular stop, whilst Hydra harbour permits no cars, preserving its charm. The bay of Aegina is sheltered and perfect for a leisurely cruise. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Saronic Gulf?

Prices vary by boat type, season and duration, from €80 for a shared tour to over €3,000 for a crewed yacht per day. A small motorboat for a half-day starts at €180. A full-day catamaran charter for six people averages €750. For a fully crewed luxury motor yacht, expect €2,500 to €4,000 per day. These prices are typical for 2026. You can book a charter online through BoatHire24 to compare options. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

  • Shared tour: from €80 per person for a 4-hour trip
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €180 for 3 people
  • Catamaran full-day: from €750 for 6 to 8 guests
  • Crewed motor yacht: from €2,500 per day for up to 10 guests

Which boat to rent in Saronic Gulf?

The best boat depends on your group size, budget and desire for speed or comfort. Here are four popular choices for the Saronic Gulf.

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 m), ideal for day trips to Aegina or nearby coves; up to 6 guests; from €180 half-day.
  2. Sailboat (30, 40 ft), perfect for a relaxed cruise to Hydra or Spetses; up to 6 guests; from €350 full-day.
  3. Catamaran (38, 45 ft), stable and spacious for families; up to 10 guests; from €750 full-day.
  4. Luxury Motor Yacht (e.g. Princess F70), for a premium experience with crew; up to 12 guests; from €2,500 per day. Check this motor yacht listing for detailed specifications.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Saronic Gulf?

Greek law requires a licence for boats over 30 hp, but many rental companies include a skipper or offer bareboat with a valid ICC. For small motorboats under 30 hp, no licence is needed. For larger boats, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is generally accepted. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. You can also see more at browse the listings.

A local tip: book a late afternoon charter from Flisvos Marina to watch the sunset over the Saronic Gulf, the sea is calm and the light is magical.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Saronic Gulf?

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months. The prevailing meltemi wind can affect the Saronic Gulf in August, but it is usually lighter than in the Cyclades. May and October offer pleasant weather and lower prices, making them a good choice for a quieter trip.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80 to €4,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip: 4 to 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 6 to 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Saronic Gulf

Can I rent a boat in Saronic Gulf without a licence?

Yes, for small motorboats up to 30 hp, no licence is required, and many operators offer skippered charters for larger vessels.

How many days do I need to explore the Saronic Gulf?

A 3-day itinerary allows you to visit Aegina, Poros and Hydra, with cruising distances of around 15 to 25 nautical miles between islands.

What is the best island to visit from Athens?

Hydra is a favourite for its car-free cobbled streets and clear swimming spots, just a 90-minute ferry from Piraeus or a short sail from the mainland.

Are there all-inclusive boat rental options?

Many crewed charters include fuel, skipper, hostess and meals; check the listing details on BoatHire24 for what is provided.

Whether you are planning a day trip or a week-long island hop, the Saronic Gulf offers some of the most accessible boating in Greece. Browse the listings on BoatHire24 to compare verified boats and prices, or list your own vessel and become a host. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Saronic Gulf or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Saronic Gulf and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
